extra.message: User-controlled input `username` (from query parameter on line 8) is directly concatenated into a Jinja2 template string passed to `Environment().from_string().render()`. An attacker can inject Jinja2 template expressions (e.g., {{7*7}}, {{config}}, or RCE payloads like {{''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__()}}) which are evaluated server-side. This enables arbitrary code execution on the server. The safe implementation using variable substitution (commented out on line 17) should be used instead.

The rendered template output (which incorporates unsanitized user input `username`) is returned as an HTMLResponse without any HTML escaping. Even without Jinja2 template injection, a simple username like `<script>alert(1)</script>` would be rendered as HTML in the victim's browser, enabling reflected Cross-Site Scripting.
